column1: POCKET AI GUARDIAN -- COMPLETE PROJECT TASK SPLIT SHEET
Vision AI Platform  |  Hardware-First Edge AI  |  Author: Youhana Sheriff  |  Last Updated: March 2026

column6: Description
column14: Notes / Risks

column6: Evaluated RDK X5 vs Qualcomm QCS6490; rejected RDK X5 due to power/thermal constraints; selected QCS6490 for edge AI portability.
column14: Decision documented in IDEOLOGY.md. QCS chosen for NPU/ISP, OTA support, thermal envelope.

column6: Designed 3-layer AI architecture: Layer 1 (Core Perception - stable), Layer 2 (Contextual Intelligence - OTA), Layer 3 (Feedback & Assistance).
column14: Modular, OTA-updatable intelligence model. Core perception models prioritise safety & stability.

column6: Defined 7 modes: Guardian, Assist, Activity (core) + Focus/Safety, Learning, Home Awareness, Drive Safety (add-on OTA).
column14: Only one mode active at a time to manage power budget.

column6: Defined Magnetic Guardian Puck: ~40x40 mm board, <5 mm thick, <20 g, passive cooling, USB-C + magnetic dock.
column14: Optional accessories: clip-on module (gym/sports), tabletop base, charging dock.

column6: Defined single universal camera: 8-12 MP, 110-120 deg wide angle, fixed focus, MIPI CSI-2.
column14: One camera simplifies supply chain and QA. Supports all modes.

column6: Hardware sold once; intelligence evolves via OTA; optional premium features; no cloud dependency; privacy as core differentiator.
column14: Documented in IDEOLOGY.md section 13.

column6: Created environment.yml (Python 3.12, all dependencies). Installed Miniforge via Homebrew for Apple Silicon compatibility.
column14: Activation: conda activate pocket_ai.

column6: Confirmed Ultralytics YOLO11, ONNX runtime, Metal Performance Shaders (MPS) acceleration working on Apple Silicon.
column14: Workers set to 0 for MPS optimisation. AMP enabled.

column5: Select YOLO architecture for edge deployment
column6: Evaluated YOLO11 family; selected YOLO11n (Nano) for sub-20 MB footprint and real-time edge inference on QCS class hardware.
column14: YOLO11n: 5.49 MB final model size. Well within <20 MB target.

column6: Acquired IndoorObstacle Detection v9i (YOLOv11 format) from Roboflow as primary training source for obstacle & person detection.
column14: Dataset biased toward static/side-profile humans; ego-centric gap identified.

column6: Ran 1-epoch preliminary training. Results: mAP@50=44.1%, Precision=30.4%, Recall=56.6%. Identified Walking Blind Spot failure mode.
column14: Model feasibility confirmed. Accuracy insufficient for production.

column5: Recover from model weight deletion incident
column6: Previous model weights were accidentally deleted. Training fully restarted from scratch on 5 Feb 2026.
column14: Run ID: indoor_obstacle_yolo11n_150ep_20260205. Full recovery confirmed.

column5: Resolve GPU OOM crash during training
column6: Batch size of 16 caused GPU memory exhaustion on M-series. Reduced to 8; training stabilised.
column14: Interim results at Epoch 57: mAP@50=75.4%, Precision=85.8%, Recall=61.5%.

column6: Full 150-epoch training on Apple Silicon (MPS). Final: mAP@50=74.72%, Precision=80.74%, Recall=68.40%, Model=5.49 MB.
column14: Best checkpoint at Epoch 46 (mAP@50=80.03%). Cosine LR annealing; patience=30.

column5: Evaluate frontal Walking Blind Spot post-training
column6: Independent evaluation of frontal-walking person detection. Recall improved to 68.40% but ego-centric gap not yet closed.
column14: Dataset bias toward side-profile poses. JRDB/SCAND datasets being curated.

column5: Export YOLO model to ONNX format
column6: Export trained YOLO11n weights (best.pt) to ONNX for cross-platform inference and Qualcomm edge deployment preparation.
column14: Required before CoreML/TFLite/QCS export. Qualcomm-optimized ONNX target <15MB.

column6: Quantize ONNX model to INT8 precision targeting Qualcomm QCS NPU. Validate accuracy drop < 2% mAP.
column14: Quantised model required for NPU/DSP execution on QCS6490.

column5: Export model to CoreML and TensorFlow Lite
column6: Convert ONNX model to CoreML (Apple) and TFLite for benchmarking on target devices. Previous CoreML EP showed ~60% graph support.
column14: Full-model CoreML conversion needed. Benchmark inference on QCS6490.

column6: Validate YOLO inference on QCS6490 NPU. Confirm model <15MB, 15-25 FPS, <600mW power.
column14: Success Criteria: Model <15MB, 15-25 FPS, <600mW.

column6: Implemented Depth-Anything-V2-Small (INT8 ONNX, 26 MB). Added ~430 ms/frame latency; exceeded <200 ms budget. Disabled in orchestrator.
column14: BB area heuristic used as fallback. CoreML EP rejected (5x slower).

column5: Optimise depth inference to meet latency budget
column6: Target: <200 ms total pipeline. Approaches: async inference on alternating frames, smaller resolution, full-model CoreML.
column14: Current: ~25 ms YOLO only. Depth adds ~430 ms. Needs 20x improvement.

column6: Replaced pyttsx3 with native macOS say command. Latest-wins single-slot design; 2-sec cooldown on all classes.
column14: Files: pipeline/tts.py, pipeline/instructor.py. Stable, no speech truncation.

column6: Evaluate Piper-TTS / Coqui TTS / Kokoro-82M as replacements for macOS say for naturalness and cross-platform support.
column14: Required for production device (non-macOS). Piper-TTS: fast offline TTS.

column5: Integrate offline TTS on QCS (<200MB RAM)
column6: Integrate selected TTS engine onto QCS6490 hardware within 200MB RAM budget. Validate latency and quality.
column14: TTS must run fully offline on device. Memory budget strict.

column6: Live webcam to YOLO detection to spatial processing to spoken instructions + GUI preview. Fully offline, real-time. ~25 ms, ~40 FPS.
column14: Status PASS vs <200 ms target. Files: main.py, pipeline/orchestrator.py, config.py.

column6: Build rule-based generator for contextual spoken instructions. Map detection outputs to natural-language templates.
column14: Generates spoken instructions from detection + spatial data.

column6: Port complete Python/C++ detection pipeline from MacBook M4 development to Qualcomm QCS6490 target hardware.
column14: Python/C++ pipeline. Requires QCS dev kit and NPU SDK.

column6: Integrate and validate MIPI CSI-2 camera interface on QCS6490. Target 30 FPS capture.
column14: Camera: 8-12 MP, 110-120 deg wide angle, fixed focus.

column6: Integrate microphone and speaker hardware on QCS6490 for TTS output and future voice input.
column14: Required for spoken alerts and voice commands.

column6: Download JRDB (JackRabbot Dataset). Filter with FiftyOne for frontal-view persons, ~1 m robot-height perspective, walking/approaching.
column14: Priority: fills ego-centric gap. Robot-height POV matches Guardian Puck mounting.

column6: Download SCAND dataset (corridor & crowd walking scenarios). Filter for frontal-walking and crowd scenarios for blind navigation.
column14: Complements JRDB. Focus on hallway and social navigation contexts.

column5: Fine-tune YOLO11n with curated ego-centric data
column6: Merge curated JRDB/SCAND samples with existing dataset. Fine-tune best.pt weights to eliminate Walking Blind Spot.
column14: Quality over volume. Prioritise frontal-view approaching persons under indoor lighting.

column6: Run independent evaluation of fine-tuned model on ego-centric test set. Target: recall >= 80% for frontal-walking class.
column14: Compare against 68.40% baseline recall from 150-epoch run.

column14: registry.json schema for all AI models across modes.

column6: Implement load/unload model mechanism scoped per operating mode. Only active mode models in memory.
column14: Manages memory budget. Critical for power management.
